content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission

April 12, 1988


Laurie Cassady
Assistant Registrar of Voters
Butte County Elections Department
25 County Center Drive
Oroville, CA  95965

Re:  Your Request for Advice
Our File No. A-88-106

Dear Ms. Cassady:

I have received your letter requesting advice on the
disclosure provisions of the Political Reform Act 1/.

QUESTION

May members of the Butte County Local Agency Formation
Commission and members of the Butte County Association of
Governments file oath forms in lieu of their annual statements
of economic interests.

CONCLUSION

A designated employee must file a signed and verified
statement of economic interests each time a statement of
economic interests is required by his or her agency's conflict
of interest code.  A designated employee may, however, file a
copy with an original signed verification, rather than
preparing a new original statement.

FACTS

You are the assistant registrar of voters for Butte County
and as such are the filing officer for statements of economic
interests for the county's designated employees.

_______________________

1/  Government Code Sections 81000-91015.  All statutory
references are to the Government Code unless otherwise
indicated.  Commission regulations appear at 2 California Code
of Regulations Section 18000, et seq.  All references to
regulations are to Title 2, Division 6 of the California Code
of Regulations.


428 J Street, Suite 800  ●  P.O. Box 807  ●  Sacramento CA 95804-0807  ●  (916) 322-5660

Laurie Cassady
April 12, 1988
Page 2


     You have indicated to me by phone that some members of the
local agency formation commission and the association of
governments are filing an oath in lieu of filing a completed
statement of economic interests. I advised you at that time
that such a filing was not appropriate and you should require
those designated employees to file completed statements of
economic interests. At my request, you sent me a sample of the
in lieu form and have asked for written advice concerning its
use.

     The annual statement oath form, which you furnished,
declares under penalty of perjury that the employee has no
further interests made reportable under the conflict of
interest code for that particular agency other than those
interests set forth in the attached copy of the most recent
statement of economic interests filed with the Fair Political
Practices Commission. In your letter you stated that some
members are filing the annual oath without attaching the most
recently filed statement of economic interests.

     Mr. Bill Turpin of the county's planning department
contacted me shortly after our telephone conversation and
indicated that the annual oath form has been used by the county
for a number of years and that he had received prior approval
from the Commission to use such a form. He indicated further
that it has been his understanding that the form is to be
attached to the designated employee's completed statement of
economic interests. There is no record in our advice files
that such advice was ever given.

                           DISCUSSION

     Every government agency must adopt a conflict of interest
code which specifically enumerates the positions within the
agency which involve the making or participation in the making
of decisions which may have an effect on any financial
interest. (Section 87300.) The law further requires that
persons holding such designated positions shall file statements
disclosing those investments, business positions, interests in
real property and income which the designated employees can
affect in their decision-making capacities. (Sections
87300-87313.)

     The financial disclosure forms (statements of economic
interests) must be signed under penalty of perjury and verified
by the filer. The verification states that the filer has used
all reasonable diligence in preparation of the statement, and
to the best of his or her knowledge, it is true and complete.

Laurie Cassady
April 12, 1988
Page 3


(Section 81004.)  The statements must be on forms prescribed by
the Commission.  (Section 83113(a).)2/

     The Commission does allow designated employees who are
required to file statements under any other agency's code, or
under Article 2 for a different jurisdiction, to expand their
statements to cover reportable interests in both jurisdic-
tions.  These officials may file copies of the expanded
statement in both jurisdictions in lieu of filing separate and
distinct original statements.  Each expanded statement must be
signed and verified by the designated employee as if it were an
original.  (Regulation 18730(b)(3).)

     The Commission does not require designated employees who
file expanded statements to also attach an oath under penalty
of perjury.  The signed verification in the statement is made
under penalty of perjury.  Those designated employees who have
filed signed and verified statements of economic interests have
been attaching the oath to the statements unnecessarily.
Designated employees may not, however, file the oath in lieu of
signed and verified statements of economic interests.

     I trust this letter has adequately explained the law's
requirements.  Your county should appreciate the conscientious
effort you are making to ensure that its public officials are
complying with the disclosure provisions of the Political
Reform Act.

     If I can be of any further assistance, please feel free to
call me at (916) 322-5901.

                                        Sincerely,

                                        Diane Griffiths
                                        General Counsel
                                        [signature]
                                        By:  Jeanette Turvill
                                        Legal Assistant
                                        Legal Division

Enclosure
cc:  Bill Turpin
     Butte County Planning Department

_____________________
     2/  See enclosed copies of our correspondence with Los
Angeles County on this subject.

BUTTE COUNTY ELECTIONS DEPARTMENT
25 COUNTY CENTER DRIVE, OROVILLE, CA 95965
916/538-7762

MARCH 9, 1988

JEANETTE TURVILL
LEGAL ASSISTANT, LEGAL DIVISION
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
P O BOX 807
SACRAMENTO, CA 95804-0807

DEAR JEANETTE:

PER OUR PHONE CONVERSATION REGARDING THE ATTACHED ANNUAL
STATEMENT OATH FORM THAT THE PLANNING DEPARTMENT DEVELOPED ABOUT
4 YEARS AGO FOR LOCAL AGENCY FORMATION COMMISSION MEMBERS AND
BUTTE COUNTY ASSOCIATION OF GOVERNMENT MEMBERS, I NEED AN OPINION
FROM YOUR OFFICE IF THIS TYPE OF DOCUMENT CAN BE USED.

SOME MEMBERS ARE FILING THIS DOCUMENT WITHOUT COPY OF "MOST
RECENT STATEMENT OF ECONOMIC INTEREST" AND SOME MEMBERS ARE
ATTACHING "MOST RECENT STATEMENT OF ECONOMIC INTEREST".

I ASKED BILL TURPIN OF THE PLANNING DEPARTMENT TO SHOW
JUSTIFICATION OF FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION ALLOWING
SUCH DOCUMENT TO BE USED. HE ASSURED ME AGAIN THAT THE FAIR
POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION ALLOWED THIS DOCUMENT TO BE USED.
I HAVE NOT HEARD FROM HIM, REGARDING THE JUSTIFICATION.

THE APRIL 1ST DEADLINE FOR FILING ANNUAL STATEMENTS IS COMING UP.
WHAT AM I TO DO WITH FILING REQUIREMENTS?????????????

PLEASE CALL ME IF YOU NEED FURTHER INFORMATION.

YOURS VERY TRULY,

LAURIE CASSADY
ASST. REGISTRAR OF VOTERS

CC: BILL TURPIN, BUTTE COUNTY PLANNING DEPT.
7 COUNTY CENTER DRIVE, OROVILLE, CA 95965

ANNUAL STATEMENT

I declare under penalty of perjury that I have no

further interests which are made reportable by the Conflict

of Interest Code of the Butte County Association of Govern-

ments than those interests, if any, set forth in the attached

copy of my most recent Statement of Economic Interests filed

with the Fair Political Practices Commission.


Executed on [illegible], 1988, at Oroville, CA.


Signature [illegible]

BUTTE COUNTY ELECTIONS DEPARMENT
25 COUNTY CENTER DRIVE, OROVILLE, CA 95965
916/538-7762
* * *

MARCH 9, 1988

JEANETTE TURVILL
LEGAL ASSISTANT, LEGAL DIVISION
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
P O BOX 807
SACRAMENTO, CA 95804-0807

DEAR JEANETTE:

PER OUR PHONE CONVERSATION REGARDING THE ATTACHED ANNUAL
STATEMENT OATH FORM THAT THE PLANNING DEPARTMENT DEVELOPED ABOUT
4 YEARS AGO FOR LOCAL AGENCY FORMATION COMMISSION MEMBERS AND
BUTTE COUNTY ASSOCIATION OF GOVERNMENT MEMBERS, I NEED AN OPINION
FROM YOUR OFFICE IF THIS TYPE OF DOCUMENT CAN BE USED.

SOME MEMBERS ARE FILING THIS DOCUMENT WITHOUT COPY OF "MOST
RECENT STATEMENT OF ECONOMIC INTEREST" AND SOME MEMBERS ARE
ATTACHING "MOST RECENT STATEMENT OF ECONOMIC INTEREST".

I ASKED BILL TURPIN OF THE PLANNING DEPARTMENT TO SHOW
JUSTIFICATION OF FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION ALLOWING
SUCH DOCUMENT TO BE USED. HE ASSURED ME AGAIN, THAT THE FAIR
POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION ALLOWED THIS DOCUMENT TO BE USED.
I HAVE NOT HEARD FROM HIM, REGARDING THE JUSTIFICATION.

THE APRIL 1ST DEADLINE FOR FILING ANNUAL STATEMENTS IS COMING UP.
WHAT AM I TO DO WITH FILING REQUIREMENTS????????????

PLEASE CALL ME IF YOU NEED FURTHER INFORMATION.

YOURS VERY TRULY,

LAURIE CASSADY
ASST. REGISTRAR OF VOTERS

CC: BILL TURPIN, BUTTE COUNTY PLANNING DEPT.
7 COUNTY CENTER DRIVE, OROVILLE, CA 95965

ANNUAL STATEMENT

I declare under penalty of perjury that I have no

further interests which are made reportable by the Conflict

of Interest Code of the Butte County Association of Govern-

ments than those interests, if any, set forth in the attached

copy of my most recent Statement of Economic Interests filed

with the Fair Political Practices Commission.


Executed on [illegible], 1988, at [illegible], CA

Signature: [illegible]

California
Fair Political
Practices Commission
March 16, 1988


Laurie Cassady
Registrar of Voters
Butte County Elections Department
25 County Center Drive
Oroville, CA  95965

Re:  88-106

Dear Ms. Cassady:

     Your letter requesting advice under the Political Reform
Act was received on March 14, 1988 by the Fair Political
Practices Commission.  If you have any questions about your
advice request, you may contact Jeanette Turvill, in the Legal
Division, directly at (916) 322-5901.

     We try to answer all advice requests promptly.  Therefore,
unless your request poses particularly complex legal questions,
or more information is needed, you should expect a response
within 21 working days if your request seeks formal written
advice.  If more information is needed, we will contact you
shortly to advise you as to the information needed.  If your
request is for informal assistance, we will answer it as
quickly as we can.  (See Commission Regulation 18329 (2 Cal.
Code of Regs. Sec. 18329).)

     You also should be aware that your letter and our response
are public records which may be disclosed to the public upon
receipt of a proper request for disclosure.

                                        Very truly yours,


                                        Diane M. Griffiths
                                        General Counsel
